records.ety: [OE. scof; akin to OFries. schof, OHG. scoph, Icel. skaup, and perh. to E. shove.]

defs: Derision; ridicule; mockery; derisive or mocking expression of scorn, contempt, or reproach.
quotes: With scoffs, and scorns, and contumelious taunts.

defs: An object of scorn, mockery, or derision.
quotes: The scoff of withered age and beardless youth.

records.ety: [Cf. Dan. skuffe to deceive, delude, Icel. skopa to scoff, OD. schoppen. See Scoff, n.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Scoffed (?; 115); p. pr. & vb. n. Scoffing.]

defs: To show insolent ridicule or mockery; to manifest contempt by derisive acts or language; -- often with at.
quotes: Truth from his lips prevailed with double sway, And fools who came to scoff, remained to pray.
God's better gift they scoff at and refuse.

defs: To treat or address with derision; to assail scornfully; to mock at.
quotes: To scoff religion is ridiculously proud and immodest.
